title: papaver dubium var. subintegrum
intro: Papaver dubium var. subintegrum, also known as the small-flowered poppy or small poppy, is a variety of the Papaver dubium plant, which is a species of poppy native to Europe and Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to induce sleep and relieve anxiety, due to its high content of alkaloids such as protopine and allocryptopine. The health benefits of Papaver dubium var. subintegrum include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory problems, making it a popular herbal remedy for colds and coughs. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used as a tea or tincture, or made into capsules or tablets, although its use should be cautious and under the guidance of a healthcare professional due to its potential side effects.
